entry.title: Fly Client Proposal
entry.summary:
STARK proofs, known for their post-quantum security, operate primarily on polynomials and hash functions without the need for trusted setups, unlike SNARKs. This foundation contributes to their classification as scalable Transparent arguments of knowledge. These proofs are relatively straightforward in their conceptualization, bearing similarities to FlyClient in their procedural genesis. Both STARKs and FlyClient initiate from an interactive proof framework; however, they diverge as FlyClient applies the Fiat-Shamir heuristic specifically to a sum of Work function, whereas STARKs extend this application to prove arbitrary functions that typically represent a CPU instruction set.
Moreover, it seems unlikely that future modifications in the form of soft forks will occur, especially those affecting script operations such as string concatenation. This indicates a stability in the current consensus mechanisms, which does not necessitate changes for the integration or application of these cryptographic proofs. This information underscores the robustness and versatility of STARK proofs in cryptographic processes and blockchain technology.
